info.name: ECUBE API
info.description:
Evidence Copying & USB Based Export — Full API Collection.

Set the `base_url` variable (default: http://localhost:8000) and authenticate via the Auth > Login request to populate the `token` variable automatically.

**Prerequisites:** The ECUBE OS groups (`ecube-admins`, `ecube-managers`, `ecube-processors`, `ecube-auditors`) must exist on the host before running the Admin — OS Users / OS Groups folders. Run `/setup/initialize` first, or create them manually (`sudo groupadd ecube-admins` etc.). If groups are missing, those requests will return 422 (Unprocessable Entity).

request.description: Basic process health endpoint. Returns 200 OK with status=ok.

exec: pm.test('Health live response has status alive and timestamp', function () {

request.description: Liveness probe endpoint. Returns 200 OK with status=alive and timestamp.

exec: pm.test('Health ready response has status and timestamp', function () {

request.description: Readiness probe endpoint. Returns 200 when dependencies are ready, 503 otherwise.
Returns application and API version. No auth required.

exec: pm.test('Public auth config contains demo_mode_enabled', function () {

request.description: Return public-safe login metadata for demo deployments. No auth required.

exec: // When a token is already injected (CI via --env-var), skip login assertions.
// pm.variables.get checks all scopes: environment, collection, and global.
pm.test('Login skipped — token already injected', function () {

request.description:
Authenticate with OS credentials. Returns a signed JWT Bearer token.
The token is automatically saved to the collection `token` variable.
Check if system has been initialized with an admin user.
Create OS groups, admin user, and seed DB with admin role. Returns 409 if already initialized.
Test PostgreSQL connectivity. Unauthenticated during initial setup; admin-required after initialization.
Create app user/database and run Alembic migrations. Returns 409 if already provisioned.
Verify current database state and connectivity. Requires admin role.
Update database configuration. Requires admin role.
Report whether the application database is already provisioned.
Runtime hints for setup wizard defaults and host context.

exec: pm.test('Drive items expose device and serial fields when present', function () {

query.description: Repeatable state filter: DISCONNECTED, AVAILABLE, IN_USE, ARCHIVED
Include DISCONNECTED drives in the default listing when no explicit state filter is used

request.description: List USB drives with state and project bindings. Supports optional project_id, repeatable state filters, and include_disconnected=true. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.
Trigger USB discovery sync, update topology, and recompute drive states. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the drive to initialize

request.description: Bind a mounted drive to a project and transition it from AVAILABLE to IN_USE. Requires a recognized filesystem, a mounted destination drive, and a MOUNTED project source share. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the drive to format

request.description: Format drive with specified filesystem (ext4 or exfat). Drive must be AVAILABLE and unmounted. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the drive to mount

request.description: Mount a drive to the managed ECUBE mount root. The drive must have a recognized filesystem and be in AVAILABLE or IN_USE state. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the drive to prepare for ejection

request.description: Complete copy/verify and transition drive back to AVAILABLE for safe removal. Roles: admin, manager.
List all registered network mounts and connectivity status. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.

Register a new network mount (SMB/NFS). Required fields: type, remote_path, project_id. Optional: username, password, credentials_file. The response includes the managed local_mount_point assigned by ECUBE. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the mount to remove

request.description: Delete mount configuration and credentials. Returns 204. Roles: admin, manager.
Test connectivity for all mounts and update status. Roles: admin, manager.

variable.description: ID of the mount to validate

request.description: Test connectivity for a specific mount. Roles: admin, manager.

query.description: Optional relative subdirectory within the mount root

request.description: List directory contents for an active USB drive or registered network mount root. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.

query.description: Maximum number of jobs to return (1-1000)

request.description: List the most recent export jobs ordered by creation time descending. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.

Create an export job with an explicit drive_id. The drive must be AVAILABLE and its current_project_id must match or be null. If the drive is currently unbound, it is automatically bound to the requested project. Starts in PENDING state. Optional callback_url (HTTPS only) receives a POST when the job reaches COMPLETED or FAILED. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

Create an export job without drive_id to auto-assign a drive. If exactly one AVAILABLE drive is bound to the project, it is selected (409 if the drive is temporarily unavailable — retry). If none are bound, an unbound AVAILABLE drive is used and the project is assigned to it. Returns 409 if multiple project-bound drives exist (specify drive_id) or if no usable drive can be acquired for the requested project. Optional callback_url (HTTPS only) receives a POST when the job reaches COMPLETED or FAILED. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the export job

request.description: Retrieve job state, progress, timestamps, file counts, drive info, and error summary. Embedded drive metadata includes the port-based device field as drive.port_system_path when available. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.

variable.description: ID of the job to update

request.description: Update a non-active job from the Job Detail workflow. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the pending job to delete

request.description: Delete a job that has not yet started. Only PENDING jobs may be removed. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the job to start

request.description: Launch copy process in background. State transitions to RUNNING. Sets started_by to the authenticated user and records started_at timestamp. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the running job to pause

request.description: Request a safe pause for an actively running export job. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the paused or otherwise safe non-active job to complete

request.description: Manually mark a safe non-active job as completed and record an audit entry. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the job to verify

request.description: Verify copied data integrity (hash comparison). State: VERIFYING → COMPLETED or FAILED. Response includes files_succeeded, files_failed, and error_summary. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

variable.description: ID of the job to generate manifest for

request.description: Generate JSON manifest on USB drive with file hashes and copy metadata. Response includes full job state with enriched fields. Roles: admin, manager, processor.

query.description: Maximum number of file rows to return (1-2000)

variable.description: ID of the job

request.description: Retrieve operator-safe file status rows for a specific export job. Roles: admin, manager, processor, auditor.

variable.description: ID of the export file

request.description: Return MD5 and SHA-256 hashes for a single export file. Roles: admin, auditor.
Compare two files by hash, size, and relative path. Roles: admin, auditor.
Inventory of USB drives: IDs, capacity, state, project bindings. Roles: all authenticated.
Introspect USB hubs/ports from sysfs. Roles: all authenticated.
List kernel block devices (disks, partitions). Roles: all authenticated.
List mounted filesystems with types and options (sensitive keys redacted). Roles: all authenticated.
Check database and job engine health. Roles: all authenticated.

Return audit logs with optional filters. Supports project_id and drive_id in addition to user, action, job_id, and time range. client_ip is redacted for manager callers. Roles: admin, manager, auditor.

exec: pm.test('Chain of custody report returns an expected status', function () {

query.description: Project selector when querying by project

request.description: Return chain-of-custody report sections by drive or project selectors. Selector precedence is drive_id, then drive_sn, then project_id. Roles: admin, manager, auditor.

exec: pm.test('Record handoff returns an expected status', function () {

request.description: Record a legal custody transfer as an append-only audit event. Roles: admin, manager.
List available log files with metadata (size, timestamps). Requires admin role.

query.description: Allowlisted log source name, for example app
Maximum number of redacted log lines to return (1-1000)
Number of newest matching lines to skip

request.description: Return recent redacted log lines from an allowlisted source. Requires admin role.

variable.description: Log filename (basename only; path traversal is rejected)

request.description: Download a specific log file. Protected against path traversal. Requires admin role.
List all USB ports with their enabled state. Requires admin or manager role.

variable.description: USB port ID

request.description: Enable or disable a USB port for ECUBE use. Requires admin or manager role.

variable.description: USB port ID

request.description: Set or update the friendly_label on a USB port. Requires admin or manager role.
List all USB hubs with enriched hardware metadata. Requires admin or manager role.

variable.description: USB hub ID

request.description: Set or update the location_hint label on a USB hub. Requires admin or manager role.
List OS users (ECUBE-relevant groups only). Role: admin.
Create OS user, set password, add to groups, and optionally assign ECUBE roles. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username to delete. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Delete OS user and remove DB role assignments. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username whose password to reset. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Reset user's OS password. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username whose groups to set. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Replace user's ECUBE groups (non-ECUBE groups preserved). Role: admin.

variable.description: Username to add groups to. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Append user to groups without removing existing memberships. Role: admin.
List OS groups (ECUBE-relevant names only). Role: admin.
Create OS group on host system. Role: admin.

variable.description: Group name to delete. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Delete OS group. Role: admin.
List all users with ECUBE role assignments. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username to get roles for. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Get role assignments for a specific user. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username to set roles for. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Replace all role assignments for a user. Role: admin.

variable.description: Username to remove all roles from. Must match pattern: ^[a-z_][a-z0-9_-]{0,31}$

request.description: Remove all role assignments for a user. Role: admin.
Read effective runtime configuration values. Role: admin.
Update one or more runtime configuration fields. Role: admin.
Restart the ECUBE app service after configuration changes. Role: admin.
Ingest frontend navigation telemetry events for troubleshooting.
